title: chrysanthemum leucanthemum var. alpicola
intro: Chrysanthemum leucanthemum var. alpicola, also known as the alpine daisy, is a subspecies of the white daisy that belongs to the chrysanthemum family of flowering plants. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to possess medicinal properties such as soothing skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from headaches and other types of pain. Chrysanthemum leucanthemum var. alpicola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for consumption, making it a versatile and effective natural remedy.
